title: verbascum vacillans
intro: Verbascum vacillans, also known as the Western or Large Mullein, is a biennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its large, hairy leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of Verbascum vacillans have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to their high mucilage content. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including soothing respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as providing relief from skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Verbascum vacillans include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
